subject: Mathematics
title: The Platonic Solids
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Platonic Solids.

hook.text: Only five 3D shapes have all faces identical and all angles equal. Just five, no more.

buildup.text: They are the tetrahedron, cube, octahedron, dodecahedron, and icosahedron.

discovery.text: Plato matched each to a classical element: earth, fire, air, water, and the cosmos.

twist.text: Euclid's Elements ends with a proof that exactly five Platonic solids exist. That's the grand finale.

climax.text: Viruses, crystals, and geodesic domes all echo these five perfect shapes.

punchline.text: Five perfect solids, and the universe uses them all.
